name: Clare Bank, National Association
description_short: Community bank with 8 locations across Southwest Wisconsin offering checking, savings, mortgages, debit cards, and online banking services.
description_long:
Clare Bank, National Association is a regional community bank headquartered in Platteville, Wisconsin, serving Southwest Wisconsin with eight convenient branch locations across Platteville, Bloomington, Boscobel, Fennimore, Kieler, and Montfort. The bank has established itself as a local financial institution focused on personalized service and accessibility to its rural and small-town customer base.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM and debit card access, online banking with bill pay functionality, and a notable emphasis on in-house mortgage lending. Clare Bank provides mortgage options including 1, 3, and 5-year balloon mortgages as well as 10 and 15-year fixed mortgages, with the bank advertising fast approval, competitive rates, low closing costs, and hassle-free loan modifications. Additional services include online check ordering, e-statements, account transfers, balance inquiries, and the ability to make deposits via debit card at ATMs.

Clare Bank distinguishes itself through its hyperlocal presence and commitment to physical branch accessibility in an increasingly digital banking landscape. The bank operates extended drive-up hours (opening at 7:30 a.m. for drive-up service) across multiple locations and maintains Saturday drive-up service in Platteville. Online banking, bill pay, and mortgage application services are available 24/7, bridging digital convenience with community-oriented service. The bank positions itself with the tagline "We Can Do That," suggesting willingness to work with customers on loan modifications and customized products.

As a community bank, Clare Bank is best suited for customers in Southwest Wisconsin seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making and personalized mortgage service. The limitation is geographic—the bank operates only in a defined regional area with eight branches, making it inaccessible to customers outside Southwest Wisconsin. Additionally, the website contains limited information regarding deposit insurance coverage, account fees, interest rates, minimum balance requirements, or specific loan terms, which are critical details for consumer decision-making. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM and debit card services with worldwide access
Bill pay services (transitioning to CheckFree platform in January 2026)
Account transfers between checking and savings
ATM balance inquiries and cash withdrawals
pros: 8 physical branch locations across Southwest Wisconsin with drive-up service at 4 locations
Extended drive-up hours opening at 7:30 a.m. at all drive-up locations
Saturday drive-up service in Platteville from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m.
In-house mortgage lending with 1, 3, 5-year balloon and 10, 15-year fixed options
24/7 online banking, bill pay, and mortgage application portal access
Hassle-free loan modifications explicitly mentioned as available
Debit cards usable worldwide at ATMs and retail locations with deposit capability
cons: Only serves Southwest Wisconsin—no nationwide or online-only banking option for out-of-state customers
Website lacks essential details on deposit insurance (FDIC coverage), account fees, minimum balances, and interest rates
Mortgage applications note 'not everyone will qualify' with no published underwriting criteria or rate information
Limited branch hours (8 a.m. to 5 p.m. weekdays, Saturday drive-up only in one location) compared to larger banks
Upcoming bill pay platform migration to CheckFree in January 2026 may require customers to take 'additional actions'
best_for: Southwest Wisconsin residents seeking personalized mortgage lending with local bank relationships
Small business owners and farmers in rural Wisconsin communities needing accessible branch service
Customers who value physical branch interaction and drive-up convenience over digital-only banking
Homebuyers interested in flexible mortgage terms (balloon or fixed) with in-house lending decisions
diagnosis: Clare Bank is best for customers in Southwest Wisconsin seeking community-based banking with accessible physical branches, personalized service, and in-house mortgage lending. The main caveat is that this is a strictly regional bank limited to eight Wisconsin locations, making it unsuitable for customers outside the service area or those preferring nationwide/online-only banking options.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
